These neurons innervate skeletal muscle fibers through the propagation of action potentials down their axons, stimulating skeletal muscle fibers at neuromuscular junctions. The nervous system uses acetylcholine to activate skeletal muscles.  Motor neurons are categorized into three main classes: alpha-motor neurons, gamma-motor neurons, and beta-motor neurons. Alpha-motor neurons control extrafusal muscle fibers, leading to movement

question: who has scored more goals in the premier league
model_answer: Alan Shearer holds the record for the most goals scored in the Premier League. He was also the first player to score 100 Premier League goals, achieving this in 124 appearances, which is the record for the fewest games to reach that milestone.

question: total number of mna in pakistan national assembly
model_answer: The National Assembly of Pakistan has a total of 342 members, who are referred to as Members of the National Assembly (MNAs). Of these, 272 are directly elected members. There are also 70 reserved seats for women and religious minorities. Specifically, 60 seats are reserved for women and 10 for minorities, as per Article 51 of the Constitution.  A political party needs to secure 137 seats to obtain and preserve a majority

question: where is the protien made in the cell
model_answer: Proteins are synthesized in the cytoplasm. In eukaryotes, proteasomes, which degrade proteins, are also found in the nucleus and the cytoplasm. Transcription, a part of protein biosynthesis, occurs in the cell nucleus.

question: what was the purpose of a revival meeting during the second great awakening
model_answer: During the Second Great Awakening, the purpose of a revival meeting was to inspire active members of a church body and to gain new converts [5, 9]. These meetings were a series of Christian religious services that emphasized personal salvation [7]. They were also a major mode of church expansion for Methodists and Baptists [3].
